entry.title: Re: Duplicate run of integration flow consuming event from event mesh
entry.content: Hi Sumit,

entry.title: Subscription failed for SAP build process automation
entry.content:
I am trying out free trial, but when I tried to subscribe to "SAP Build Process Automation" it failed with a message "Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community." tried four times and failed all the time.
I am following steps as per the tutorial. Is there any prerequisite to be able to get it working?

entry.title: Re: Duplicate run of integration flow consuming event from event mesh
entry.content: Hi Sumit, I have the same problem for two weeks. Might it be an SAP bug? Same published event is triggering for two times. Did you find a sloution?

entry.title: Re: AATP Product Allocation table in Fiscal Period instea...
entry.content: Thank you very much Carsten. This is very helpful.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Batch Determination Issue in EWM (S/4HANA C...
entry.content:
Hi @JoseGuill ,

the batch determination in Warehouse Management is supported. It also considers the batch selection criteria if they are defined. In case this process does not work for you, I'd suggest to create an incident.

If you have already created an incident and received a negative response, please share the incident number with me and I can have a look.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: error when recording characterist on QA32: The entered value is too high (Check the assignment)
entry.content: This is only a warning message, and it disappears when I press Enter, but it's annoying because it shows up when recording every characteristic, and I’d like to turn it off.

entry.title: How to create check logic in "Export Master Data - Business Partners"
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have two questions about "Export Master Data - Business Partners"

①

Is it possible to define check logic when registering with "Export Master Data - Business Partners"?

②

Is it possible to register data for custom fields with "Export Master Data - Business Partners"?

entry.title: Re: CO Configuration Error in OKZ3 (Default Values for Order Cost Estimate)
entry.content: Could you please tell how we can avoid this error as it does not allow to delete the entries

entry.title: Re: How to properly Bind SAPUI5 SmartTable in CAP to SAP...
entry.content: I managed to get the SmartTable to send the correct request and retrieve the expected data. However, the SmartTable still displays the input parameters PARAM1 and PARAM2 as table columns, instead of showing the actual data columns returned in the response. Do you have any idea how can I fix this?

entry.title: 提供開始：SAP Help Portal コンテンツを機械翻訳で即時に翻訳
entry.content:
SAP Help Portal に機械翻訳 (MT) 機能が導入されました。この新機能では、ドキュメントを即時に 39 の言語に翻訳できるため、任意の言語で必要な情報にアクセスすることができます。
※この記事は英語版の日本語翻訳です。併せてご覧ください。

使い方

  1. 任意の SAP Help Portal ページを開きます。
  2. ドロップダウンメニューから言語を選択します。
  3. コンテンツは、SAP Translation Hub の AI 機械翻訳を使用して、数秒で即時に翻訳されます。

フィードバックのお願い

お客様からのフィードバックは、コンテンツと翻訳のどちらを改善する上でも大変重要です。そのため、簡単にフィードバックをお寄せいただけるようにしました。

  • 各ドキュメントページの右側に、「このページは役に立ちましたか」というメッセージが表示されます。
  • 「いいえ」を選択すると、フィードバックがコンテンツの品質に関するものなのか、機械翻訳に関するものなのかを選択できます。
  • 「はい」を選択された場合、参考になった点をぜひお聞かせください。肯定的なフィードバックは、何がうまく機能しているかを弊社が理解する上で有用です。
  • 改善が必要な点について、フリーテキストコメントを追加いただくことができます。
  • SAP Help Portal にログインされている場合は、追加のコメントオプションが表示されます。電子メールアドレスを残すことで、コンテンツ作成者と改善点について直接ディスカッションすることもできます。

フィードバックへの対応について

いただいたフィードバックはコンテンツ作成者または言語エキスパートに直接送信され、必要な改善が行われます。これにより、ドキュメントの全体的な品質が向上し、より役立つものになります。

🌍今すぐ機械翻訳をお試しいただき、ご意見をお聞かせください。

🔗詳細について SAP Help Portal の新機能、SAP Translation Hub 文書翻訳 - 概要

entry.title: Sap pm dates
entry.content:
Hi, I’ve tried but I’m having trouble figuring it out because there are so many dates to choose from. I’m working in Power BI using data from SAP, which is retrieved from a Snowflake database. I want to get the same results as when searching in SAP. My question is: which dates (columns and table) does SAP use for the search?
First, I’m wondering about Notification (IW28) – which table and column does it look at for:

  1. Notification date – when I check the variant, it says DATUV (from date) and DATUB (to date).
    Next, I’m wondering about Work Order (IW38):

  2. From date (DATUV) to To date (DATUB)

entry.title: Re: SF Recruiting Management - DocuSign integration
entry.content:
Hi,

We also have the same use case, We need the support from community to understand, if anyone able to achieve this functionality.

entry.content:
Refer the blog below : 

 Update custom field created in EKKO via CFL App while creating PO using BAPI_PO_CREATE1

entry.title: Re: SAP Web IDE - All Workspace projects have just disappeared
entry.content: Hi keren_rotenberg,

entry.title: Re: How to rename the CDS view.
entry.content: In 2025 still having the same issue.

entry.title: Re: Good Receipt Production Embedded EWM
entry.content: Hi, with RF I got this error message "No open quantity found for manufacturing order..." (/SCWM/RF_EN840)

entry.title: Re: SAP Web IDE - All Workspace projects have just disappeared
entry.content: Hi keren_rotenberg, I have lost all my projects in my SAP WEB IDE Workspace after Logout. Can we restore those projects back to Workspace? can please provide the solution if we have?

entry.title: Re: Exporting Tables from Datasphere to Hana Datalake File System using Replication Flow
entry.content:
Hi Ravi,

First you need to create a CSR file using openssl. Post this, you need to get the CSR signed with Root certificate. Once done, use openssl to create p12 file (for Keystore file) and key file (for Keystore password).

Regards,

Karthik

entry.title: Material Description in Adobe Form not showing line breaks – All text appearing in single line
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I’m working on an Adobe Form for invoice printing, and I have a requirement related to the Material Description field inside the line items.

This field is a free text field — sometimes users maintain multiple lines of text using the Enter key (i.e., line breaks). For example:

 

Line 1
Line 2
Line 3
However, in the PDF output, all the text is appearing in a single line, like:
Line 1Line 2Line 3
 

My Requirement:

If the user presses Enter while maintaining the Material Description, I want the output in the PDF form to preserve those line breaks, so the result should appear like this:

Line 1
Line 2
Line 3

Request for Help:

Can anyone please suggest what changes I need to make in either:

  • The Adobe Form layout, or

  • The ABAP driver program

    to achieve this behavior?

    I want this to work dynamically — i.e., I don’t want to hardcode anything. The output should respect whatever line breaks are maintained in the source.

    Thanks in advance for your guidance! 🙏

entry.title: Re: Inbound Delivery 180000013 could not be distributed M...
entry.content: Look at SAP Services and Support for KBA such as 2775368 - Inbound delivery with account assignment not distributed to EWM.

entry.title: Re: Setup of Scan GS1 barcodes in RF
entry.content:
Hi @Frederik_S ,

please have a look at note 3364010. #6 should hopefully address your questions.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: SAP Concur: How to get Support
entry.content:
Need help with SAP Concur? Let's begin by understanding who you are and your role.



  1. Administrator Support 

If you are an Administrator or an Authorized Support Contact (ASC) responsible for managing, configuring, and overseeing your company’s SAP Concur system, dedicated support is available to assist you. 

An Authorized Support Contact (ASC) is a designated role assigned to 2-5 individuals within your organization, granting them enhanced access to SAP Concur support and resources. 

How to Access Support: 

  • Technical Assistance: Submit a request through the SAP Concur Support Portal. 
  • Phone Support: Call +1 (855) 895-4815 and select “Technical Support” (option 4). 
  • Training & Resources: Explore the SAP Concur Training Library for comprehensive guides and multilingual instructional materials. 

2. User Support 

Select this option if you use SAP Concur but do not manage or administer the system. If you do not see “Administrator” on your dashboard, you fall under this category. 
 

Advanced Technical Assistance – User Support Desk (USD) 
 

The User Support Desk (USD) is a premium SAP Concur service that provides first-level technical support. However, not all organizations subscribe to this service, as some prefer to handle support internally. 

 

To check if your company has USD access: 

  1. Log in to your SAP Concur account. 

  2. Click on the Question mark (?) icon and select “Contact Support”, or find the option at the bottom of your SAP Concur homepage. 

  3. If your company has USD, you will see the “Contact Support” button under the Question mark (?) menu, which will direct you to the User Support Desk Portal. 
  4. In the USD Portal, you can: 
  • Find support phone numbers 
  • Chat with experts 
  • Submit a help ticket for further assistance. 

For those who do not have the option to contact SAP Concur Support, please reach out to your internal support desk for assistance. You can find details about your company’s SAP Concur administrators in the Company Notes section within your SAP Concur system or through internal documentation and resource portals. 
  

For users who do not have the option to contact SAP Concur Support, assistance must be sought through your internal support desk. You can find information about your company's SAP Concur administrators in the Company Notes section within your SAP Concur system or through internal documentation and resource portals. 

If you're unsure where to start, reach out to your direct manager for guidance. In most organizations, the SAP Concur site administrator is part of the Accounting, Finance, HR, Payroll, or IT department. 

For login-related issues, refer to the following resources: 

  • How Do I Sign In to My SAP Concur Profile? 
  • Why Can’t I Sign In to the SAP Concur Tool? 

Product Interest 

Select this option if you are not an SAP Concur customer but would like to learn more about our products. 

You will be redirected to contact our Sales team, where you can fill out a form, and a representative will reach out to you. Additionally, you will find the Sales team’s contact number for further guidance on SAP Concur solutions. 

General SAP notes： 
 
SAP Concur Customer Support and Resources - SAP Concur  

SAP Concur Customer Support and Resources - SAP Concur Sweden 

Admin Support for SAP Concur Customers - SAP Concur Sweden  

SAP Concur | SAP Help Portal 

Contact a Concur Sales Representative - SAP Concur Sweden  

Support and FAQs - SAP Concur Community  

2630530 - SAP Ariba, Concur, Fieldglass, BusinessOne Support Channels - SAP for Me 

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have.  

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community  

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: Re: Awesome SAP Stammtisch Gurugram 16-April-2025 - Thank you!
entry.content: Thanks for great team work.It was inspiring to see the enthusiasm and commitment everyone displayed towards our shared objectives for Gurugram Group.As we move forward, let’s continue to support one another and keep the momentum going. Please feel free to reach out if you have any further thoughts or ideas that you would like to discuss. #sap #stammtischgurugram-ncr #gurugram #stammtisch

entry.title: Understanding Test Data within SAP S/4HANA projects
entry.content:
The success of a SAP Testing project largely depends on how well your teams understand the organization of data in SAP and where to find the right data. This blog explores the architecture of SAP data and provides practical insights into effective test data management for S/4HANA projects.

Understanding Real-World Test Data Challenge

An SAP Materials Management (MM) team encountered significant issues while testing their procure-to-pay (P2P) process. They had created synthetic purchase orders manually for their testing activities.

Issues Faced:

  • The synthetic POs lacked historical goods receipt references, rendering them invalid for invoice verification (MIRO transaction)
  • The team used outdated vendor master data (LFA1), which resulted in blocked payments in the Financial module
  • The test data failed to include multiple tax codes, causing tax mismatches between purchase orders and invoices

Lessons Learned:

  • Transactional data must be realistic and comprehensive, including all necessary historical references
  • Using production-like purchase orders is essential for validating end-to-end processes
  • Test data provisioning should be automated whenever possible to eliminate manual errors

This example illustrates how incomplete or unrealistic test data can derail testing efforts and potentially lead to incorrect conclusions about system functionality.

Understanding The Architectural Framework of SAP Data

Before diving into test data strategies, let us understand how data is organized within SAP systems.

SAP data falls into three primary categories, each with distinct characteristics and functions:

1. Master Data

Master data represents the stable entities that form the foundation of your business operations. These are the core elements that remain relatively unchanged over time.

Key Characteristics:

  • Stored in persistent core tables
  • Used across multiple SAP modules to ensure consistency

Examples of Master Data:

  • Material Master (MARA): Contains all material-specific information used across purchasing, inventory, and production processes
  • Customer Master (KNA1): Stores essential customer details for sales and billing operations
  • Vendor Master (LFA1): Holds supplier information critical for procurement processes
  • Business Partner (BP000): Contains unified business partner records in S/4HANA

Where to Access Master Data:

  • via SAP Tables: MARA, KNA1, LFA1, BP000
  • via SAP Fiori Apps: "Manage Business Partners," "Display Material"
  • via Transaction Codes: SE16N, MM03, BP

2. Transactional Data

Transactional data captures the day-to-day business activities and operations like orders that constantly change as your business evolves.

Key Characteristics:

  • More dynamic and shorter-lived than master data
  • Drives real-time business processes
  • High volume, often requiring robust archiving strategies

Examples of Transactional Data:

  • Sales Orders (VBAK/VBAP): Header and item data generated during the order process
  • Purchase Orders (EKKO/EKPO): Header and item information created during procurement
  • Financial Documents (BKPF/BSEG): Accounting document headers and line items

Where to Access Transactional Data:

  • SAP Tables: VBAK, VBAP, BKPF, BSEG, EKKO, EKPO
  • SAP Fiori Apps: "Manage Sales Orders," "Display Purchase Orders"
  • Transaction Codes: VA03, ME23N

3. Configuration Data

Configuration data refers to the settings and parameters that define how the SAP system behaves and operates according to a business’s needs. For example, setting up a Sales Document Type (ZINT) for Internet Orders.

Key Characteristics:

  • Maintained through SPRO (SAP Project Reference Object)
  • Stored in customization tables
  • Configuration data is client-specific and is migrated or transported from one system (e.g., DEV) to another (e.g., QA, PROD) using transport requests.

Examples of Configuration Data:

  • Pricing Configuration (T685): Defines how prices are determined in Sales & Distribution
  • Material Types (T134): Categorizes materials (raw materials, finished goods, etc.)
  • Company Codes (T001): Defines organizational units in Finance
  • Account Determination (T030): Controls which GL accounts receive transaction postings

Where to Access Configuration Data:

  • SAP Tables: T685, T001, T030
  • SPRO Path: Various paths depending on the configuration area
  • Transaction Codes: SPRO, OBYC

Methods to Create and Manage SAP Test Data

Once you have a clear understanding of how data is structured, you can leverage the following tools or methods to create and manage test data effectively:

Where to look for Existing SAP Test Data

it's not always necessary to create test data for testing. In many cases, we can leverage existing data already present in the system. Let us explore various methods for identifying and reusing suitable data.

Transaction Codes (T-Codes)

Transaction codes provide direct access to master data records within SAP GUI. Below are some commonly used T-codes:

  • PA20 – Display HR Master Data: View detailed employee master data across various infotypes (read-only).
  • FI03 – Manage Banks: Display bank master data after it is imported into SAP.
  • MM03 – Material Master: Access material master records.
  • MD04 – Master Data Overview: View real-time master data and stock levels.
  • VA03 – Sales Order Display: Retrieve sales order details.
  • FB05 – Financial Accounting: View financial accounting documents.

SAP Fiori Apps

SAP Fiori provides modern web-based applications to manage and display master data. Some useful apps include:

  • Manage Product Master Data (F1602): Search, view, edit, create, and copy product master data.
  • Manage G/L Account Master Data: Access G/L account details via the SAP Fiori launchpad.
  • Manage Banks: View and update bank master data.

SAP Tables

The following SAP tables in SE16/ SE11 can help with master data

  • MARA (Material Master)
  • KNA1 (Customer Master)
  • LFA1 (Vendor Master)
  • BKPF (Accounting Document Header)
  • VBAK – Sales Order Header
  • AFKO – Production Order Header

SAP Reports and Queries

  • You may use SAP Query (SQVI/ SQ01) to generate reports on master data.

You may also leverage standard SAP reports, for example, MB52, for material stock overview

Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Test Data Management

  • Begin Early: Start planning your test data strategy during the test design phase
  • Automate Where Possible: Use automation tools for data creation, masking, and subsetting
  • Document As Much As Possible: Maintain comprehensive documentation of test data

entry.title: Re: Good Receipt Production Embedded EWM
entry.content: Can you share screeen shots, pmr, production otder, rf error please.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Wien - 08.05.2025
entry.content:
Ein gemütlicher Abend in lockerer Runde - zum zwanglosen Austausch über Themen rund um SAP und mehr.
Von der SAP Community, für die SAP Community.

Tritt der Gruppe Wien bei und subscribe den Blog-Post SAP Stammtisch Wien um über zukünftige Events informiert zu werden.

Bitte um verlässliche Zusage mit der RSVP Option rechts - das erleichtert uns die Reservierung in den jeweiligen Lokalen sehr.

entry.title: RFQ Contracts PO Release Strategy Setup
entry.content:
Setup Release Strategy for External Documents

External documents release strategy is always to be set as header wise with selection of header wise tables and fields. The header table to be referred is CEKKO. The same class is to be used for Objects of the external procurement. 

 

Create Characteristics CT04: 

Triggering this Release Strategy Based upon Document Types only, however user can create characteristics based upon the requirement.  

 

Create Class – Cl02

 

Enter the characteristics in the class.

 

Release Strategy setup for RFQ

SPRO Path - SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> RFQ and Quotation (SAP ERP) -> Release Procedure for RFQs -> Define Release Procedure for RFQs

 

Setup Release Group:

Setup Release Codes -

Setup Indicators:

Setup Release Strategy:  

 

Release Prerequisites:

Classification:

 

Simulate Release:

 

Assign object to classes - Cl20N

Transaction– ME41 RFQ Creation

ME45 RFQ release -

 

Setup Release Strategy for Purchase Orders.

SPRO Path - SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Purchase Order -> > Release Procedure for Purchase Orders -> Define Release Procedure for Purchase Orders

Create a New Release Group for PO with the same defined class used for RFQ.

 

Transaction – Po Creation

Setup Release Strategy for Contracts.

SPRO Path - SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Contract-> Release Procedure for Contracts -> Define Release Procedure for Contracts

 

 

Transaction: Create Contract. 

Transaction: ME35K (Release Contracts) 

Regards,

Sagnik Chakravarty.

entry.title: Re: Sap pm dates
entry.content:
Hi,

For IW28 (Notifications), SAP filters by creation date ERDAT from table QMEL.
For IW38 (Work Orders), it filters by basic start date GSTRP from table AFKO.
Use these fields in Power BI to match SAP's DATUV/DATUB filtering.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Create BP in S4 through ECC CREMAS05 - BUT000-BPEXT
entry.content: Thanks for the information on that, while digging a bit into that i found method FILL_AND_MAINTAIN_BP_FROM_VEND where "CALL BADI lr_badi_extension->map_external_data" is called. In there i was able to find the structure/fields which need to be filled "CT_DATA-PARTNER-CENTRAL_DATA-COMMON-DATA-BP_CENTRALDATA-PARTNEREXTERNAL"

entry.title: Re: Sap pm dates
entry.content:
Hi,

For IW28 (Notifications), SAP filters by creation date (ERDAT) from table QMEL.
For IW38 (Work Orders), it filters by basic start date (GSTRP) from table AFKO.
Use these fields in Power BI to match SAP's DATUV/DATUB filtering.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Free SAP Certification and practice systems for students & lecturers
entry.content:
Hi @hotiendat117 

I’m sorry to hear that you're still experiencing issues with the activation email.

I can confirm that your account has been provisioned. You should have received an activation email in your mailbox by now. Please make sure to check your spam or junk folders just in case it was filtered there.

If you still can’t locate the email, let me know and I’ll be happy to assist further.

Looking forward to your confirmation.

Best regards,

Maria

entry.title: Purchase Order CO commitment inconsistencies
entry.content:
Dear all,

We have an issue reporting figures from one PS project. There are some CO documents posted with value zero at some Purchase Order documents on Commitment Documents level while I can see the amount at the Good Receipt CO document. 

It seems there is a discrepancy between the CO tables totals and positions so I executed SAP note 1746429. It did not change the difference between PS and FM reports on the project.

Please help me on this issue that affects the delivery of Donors reports. 

Best,

Rodolfo

entry.title: SAP DM - Roles, Spaces & Groups
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we have created roles with the role templates in BTP. Is there any possibility to delete specific apps from roles or do we need to accept that a user/user group has all apps included in a roll template?

We also asked ourselves if it is possible to create and edit Spaces & Groups in DM for whole user groups?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: CO Configuration Error in OKZ3 (Default Values for Or...
entry.content:
Hi,

Also check configuration in 0PL8.

Regards

entry.title: SAP Concur: How to get Support
entry.content:
Need help with SAP Concur? Let's begin by understanding who you are and your role.



  1. Administrator Support 

If you are an Administrator or an Authorized Support Contact (ASC) responsible for managing, configuring, and overseeing your company’s SAP Concur system, dedicated support is available to assist you. 

An Authorized Support Contact (ASC) is a designated role assigned to 2-5 individuals within your organization, granting them enhanced access to SAP Concur support and resources. 

How to Access Support: 

  • Technical Assistance: Submit a request through the SAP Concur Support Portal. 
  • Phone Support: Call +1 (855) 895-4815 and select “Technical Support” (option 4). 
  • Training & Resources: Explore the SAP Concur Training Library for comprehensive guides and multilingual instructional materials. 

2. User Support 

Select this option if you use SAP Concur but do not manage or administer the system. If you do not see “Administrator” on your dashboard, you fall under this category. 
 

Advanced Technical Assistance – User Support Desk (USD) 
 

The User Support Desk (USD) is a premium SAP Concur service that provides first-level technical support. However, not all organizations subscribe to this service, as some prefer to handle support internally. 

To check if your company has USD access: 

  1. Log in to your SAP Concur account. 

  2. Click on the Question mark (?) icon and select “Contact Support”, or find the option at the bottom of your SAP Concur homepage. 

  3. If your company has USD, you will see the “Contact Support” button under the Question mark (?) menu, which will direct you to the User Support Desk Portal. 

  4. In the USD Portal, you can: 
  • Find support phone numbers 
  • Chat with experts 
  • Submit a help ticket for further assistance. 

For those who do not have the option to contact SAP Concur Support, please reach out to your internal support desk for assistance. You can find details about your company’s SAP Concur administrators in the Company Notes section within your SAP Concur system or through internal documentation and resource portals. 
  

For users who do not have the option to contact SAP Concur Support, assistance must be sought through your internal support desk. You can find information about your company's SAP Concur administrators in the Company Notes section within your SAP Concur system or through internal documentation and resource portals. 

If you're unsure where to start, reach out to your direct manager for guidance. In most organizations, the SAP Concur site administrator is part of the Accounting, Finance, HR, Payroll, or IT department. 

For login-related issues, refer to the following resources: 

  • How Do I Sign In to My SAP Concur Profile? 
  • Why Can’t I Sign In to the SAP Concur Tool? 

Product Interest 

Select this option if you are not an SAP Concur customer but would like to learn more about our products. 

You will be redirected to contact our Sales team, where you can fill out a form, and a representative will reach out to you. Additionally, you will find the Sales team’s contact number for further guidance on SAP Concur solutions. 

General SAP notes： 
 
SAP Concur Customer Support and Resources - SAP Concur  

SAP Concur Customer Support and Resources - SAP Concur Sweden 

Admin Support for SAP Concur Customers - SAP Concur Sweden  

SAP Concur | SAP Help Portal 

Contact a Concur Sales Representative - SAP Concur Sweden

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have.

entry.title: Re: Quarterly Release Highlights: SAP BTP Security and Identity & Access Management
entry.content:
Hi Wallace, 

thank you for your comment!

We publish planned features and enhancements in the SAP Roadmap Explorer, which is the ultimate source of information for new capabilities or improvements. Release Notes contain information about newly released features. Other sources, such as this blog, serve the purpose of putting a spotlight on selected new developments.

I hope this answers your question - 

Kind regards
Regine

entry.title: Re: Trust Configuration" in SAP BTP Trial Account – Unable to Proceed with SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content: Why do you need Trust Configuration to enable Work Zone?

entry.title: SAP Process Improvement Suggestion – Payment Received at One Location, Material Sales at Multiple Lo
entry.content:
SAP Process Improvement Suggestion – Payment Received at One Location, Material Sales at Multiple Locations

In our current SAP setup, when a payment is received at a single location but the sales are made from multiple locations, the accounts team needs to manually split the payment and book multiple transactions based on the respective sales locations. This leads to extra effort, potential errors, and inefficiencies in reconciliation.

Suggested Improvement:
Introduce a "Single Click Payment Split by Location" functionality in SAP, where:

  • The system automatically identifies the sales locations related to the payment.

  • It enables the accounts team to split and assign the received amount location-wise with just one action.

  • This ensures accurate financial reporting, faster processing, and reduced manual intervention.

 Benefits:

  • Simplifies the workflow for the accounts team

  • Improves data accuracy and audit trail

  • Enhances visibility of payment allocation across locations

Would love to hear thoughts from fellow SAP users or consultants – has anyone implemented something similar?

entry.title: Re: Monitor S.O. vs outbound delivery number
entry.content:
Hi Alex16280,

Your post was incorrectly tagged with SAP Business One, we have therefore changed it for you. You can change this anytime if this tag is not correct by editing your post. Thank you for your contribution in the SAP Community!

Best regards, 

Kavya

entry.title: Universal Parallel Accounting
entry.content:
Universal parallel accounting is a new buzz by SAP being talked about from recent past. This is a topic which cut across many processes with finance and management accounting. In this post I will try to explain some concepts for better understanding of this topic.

Universal parallel accounting; as the name suggest; addresses parallel accounting requirements for multiple accounting principles. SAP regular solution also addresses parallel accounting requirements to great extent already without using universal parallel accounting. However, there are many areas where parallel accounting requirements are not addressed by default by SAP without using UPA.

Some of the motivation which leads to UPA functionality are as under:

  • Report according to different fiscal year variants e.g., local v/s group
  • Report inventory based on actual cost based LOCAL Gap. China, Brazil
  • Functional Currency different from group and country currency
  • Group Margin without intercompany profit

Following picture explain the limitation of parallel accounting without using UPA:

This view shows that Blue flow is related to group GAAP and hence no separate valuation maintained within CO. In general, this still looks fine, but it is not the case when data flows back from CO to FI (asset manufacturing and Inventory Valuation) hence manual adjustment postings were necessary due to this.

With UPA:

 

Let's understand below areas to build more understanding:

Asset Accounting: 

Settlement of Asset under construction to Final Asset per ledger. AuC Settlement generally post accounting to leading and non-leading ledgers and there is no control on settlement differently per ledger. UPA addresses this requirement whereby settlement rules are possible to create differently per ledger and finally AuC to Final asset takes place based on different settlement rules maintained per ledger.

Example:

Settle per ledger: L1 settle to asset L2 settle to Cost center.

Work around functionality available with Investment Management. This was complex and not fully accurate with IM functionality:

Settlement in UPA looks as under:

 

Inventory Valuation 

Without UPA there is only one inventory price, adjustments, delta runs at the time of period end, with introduction of UPA; inventory price is possible per ledger.

ECC/S/4 functionality without UPA was only possible at currency level but not per ledger.

With UPA it is possible to calculate, release and update price per ledger:

 

 

Actual Costing for Local GAAP

There are legal requirements for example in Brazil and China to perform actual costing for local GAAP. Without UPA actual costing is executed based on leading ledger and therefore posting takes place to both leading and non-leading ledger. With introduction of UPA it is possible to perform actual costing run per ledger.

Workaround without UPA involves below set up:

with UPA it is possible to have actual costing per ledger

 

Multiple Currency Requirements

There are 10 parallel currencies allowed in G/L whereas only 3 currencies are allowed in material ledger, 2 currencies are allowed in controlling. Some customers also have multiple controlling areas which add further complexity from currency point of view. With UPA in place 10 currencies are supported consistently through the applications like Material Ledger, Controlling, G/L and AA.

Workaround to address multiple currency requirement with limitation of translation based on current date is:

With UPA; No deviation in any sub ledger.

Decision to switch to UPA:

Once switched to UPA there is no come back, and hence proper evaluation should be performed before taking this decision. Also, there are limitations on certain functionality being in progress yet and therefore it is very important to understand the same. Refer SAP Note 

3265275 - Universal Parallel Accounting: FAQ

 

 

 

  •

entry.title: Re: How to know if the element is an OData entity
entry.content: you can find fileds the in metadata. parse edmx and check if field exist

entry.title: Re: RFC call failed: Error when opening an RFC connection...
entry.content:
Follow: 3313569 - No data collectors exist raised during the execution of RZ70 - SAP for Me

and then execute RZ70, it will do.

entry.title: Looking to Understand User Exits in SAP SD with Real-Time Case Studies
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I’m currently working in the SAP SD module and looking to expand my knowledge in the area of User Exits, specifically within the Sales and Distribution (SD) domain.

I would like to mention that I am completely new to this functionality and eager to understand it from the ground up. I’ve come across the term "User Exit" quite often in discussions related to customization and enhancements, but I’m still unclear about:

  • What exactly are User Exits and how do they differ from BADIs or Enhancements?

  • Where and how are they implemented in SAP SD?

  • What are some real-time business use cases where User Exits have been effectively used to solve specific problems or meet business requirements?

If possible, I would greatly appreciate step-by-step explanations or references to detailed examples that can help a beginner like me understand this concept in depth. Any inputs, documents, or links would be very helpful!

Thanks in advance for your time and guidance.

Best regards,
Sobhagya Thapa

entry.title: Re: Free SAP Certification and practice systems for students & lecturers
entry.content:
Hi @Guru_Charan,

Sorry for the delay and thank you for your patience!
I can see you as an active user in our system. Hope you enjoy exploring the Learning Hub and all the  available content.

Best regards,

Maria

entry.title: Is Goal library mapping Deprecated?
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

When i am trying to map goal library in goal plan and importing the goal plan in provisioning. Then i got a warning saying Goal library mapping is deprecated.

That means we cannot use library goals? 

Thanks and regards,

Vasu.

entry.title: Re: error when recording characterist on QA32: The entere...
entry.content:
Hi,

This warning occurs because the recorded value exceeds the upper limit defined in the inspection characteristic (MIC) master data or inspection plan. SAP gives you the chance to override it, but it warns every time.

Regards

entry.title: BAdi for inventory sampling
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
We have an issue that the old materials that were marked deleted long ago still appear in the ph.inventory documents after random selection.
The note below tells that it is not possible to exclude such materials unless we archive them. But archiving seems for us risky as we might need to restore materials which will not be possible after archiving.
The stock mngmt level can´t be changed in our case too.

Is there any BAdi to include the condition that "deleted" materials are not included into the random selection?

If you faced similar please share how you could overcome it.

Thank you

2096763 - Profile settings 'Include deletion flag' and 'Consider zero stock balances' in the inventory sampling process (SAP ERP & SAP S/4 HANA) - SAP for Me

entry.title: SAP Enable Now enablement: Important Updates for Consultants and Learners
entry.content:
SAP lately implemented several changes to streamline the SAP Enable Now-related enablement portfolio:

  1. Classroom Training:

    • Standard instructor-led training courses for SAP Enable Now are no longer available as of calendar week 22.
    • The lab infrastructure used for hands-on practice during these courses has been decommissioned.
    • Customers who still require training must now opt for customer-specific training sessions.
    • These customized training sessions will be conducted using the customer's own SAP Enable Now instance for hands-on exercises.

  2. E-learning Courses:

    • Existing e-learning courses (SEN100e, SEN200e, and SEN300e) will remain available on learning.sap.com.
    • However, these courses will no longer be maintained or updated.
    • For the interim solution 'SAP Enable Now - enablement content creation edition', new courses with reduced content will be provided.
    • The new course codes are SEN101e and SEN201e.
    • It's important to note that SEN300 (consulting) is not affected by these changes.

  3. Certification:

    • The consulting certification "C_SEN" has been officially retired.
    • Consultants who already hold this certification do not need to complete any "stay certified" assessments.
    • The 'certified consultant' status will be automatically extended for one year upon expiration.
    • After this extra year existing certification badges will remain visible on "Credly" and will maintain their validity until the official end-of-life of SAP Enable Now (which has not yet been scheduled). An "expired" flag will be added to show the inactive state.
    • No further action is required from certified consultants to maintain their status.

  4. Partner Enablement:

    • All partner-related enablement materials and content sources have been retired.
    • These resources are no longer available for access or use.

These changes ensure a focused approach to SAP Enable Now enablement services while accommodating the integration of WalkMe into SAP's product portfolio. Consultants with existing certifications benefit from automatic extensions and continued validity of their credentials. Customers seeking training can still receive tailored instruction using their own instances. The introduction of new e-learning courses for the interim solution "SAP Enable Now - enablement content creation edition" provides continuity in learning opportunities. It's important to note that SAP Enable Now product maintenance will continue without restrictions until further notice, and ongoing service engagements or planned implementations are not affected by these changes.

entry.title: Re: error when recording characterist on QA32: The entere...
entry.content: The result is exactly the value, which matches the upper specification limit, but the system is rejecting it as “too high.”

entry.title: Re: SCM-14003 - Allow labelling nominees as Emergency Cover

I understand there is an Icon ahead of Readiness which defines this nomination is an emegency cover. Please let me know is this a standard functionality where emergency cover only visible while adding the nomination to the position.

Steffen

entry.title: Re: creating or updating service instance: Could not update parameters of service "cpaap-xsuaa-
entry.content: Hi, I'm also getting this error. find any solution.

entry.title: How do I make the Line Item Text field reportable?
entry.content:
how do I make the Line Item Text field of sales order reportable in our custom dispatch report?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How do I make the Line Item Text field reportable?
entry.content:
Please refer to SAP KBA 0003282990

Resolution

Consider using API Sales Order (A2X) to extract the header and item texts for the sales orders:

  • /A_SalesOrderItem(SalesOrder='{SalesOrder}',SalesOrderItem='{SalesOrderItem}')/to_Text (Reads the text of a sales order item).
  • /A_SalesOrder('{SalesOrder}')/to_Text (Reads the header texts of a sales order).

 

There is currently an Influence Idea for this requirement: CDS View for Long Text. 

Within this influence request development have provided the following update:

"To offer the CDS view for long text, the text table need to be redesigned, all text data has to be migrated and the text data reading, writing, copying logic along the sales E2E process have to be updated. To make sure the replacement will work smoothly after the replacement, a proof of concept has been planned in the coming release. And the feature will be planned later based on the findings."

The next update on this will be Q2 2025.

entry.title: Re: Inbound Delivery 180000013 could not be distributed M...

entry.title: Re: Error while trying to activate new SAP key
entry.content: Hi Steffen, In that situation I'd open a case with SAP Support - good luck. Regards, Mathias

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One 10.0 FP2208: Connection to the datab...
entry.content:
Check 1 -

Login to SLD

Click on DB Instance and select the database and click on refresh database user.

Check 2 -

Copy the Database User against the company database from SLD login.

Log in into SQL/Hana database 

Select on Security-> Login-> Filter as per the database user

Right click on User and select Properties, check for status, it should be enabled.

entry.title: Re: Get Data from https://smard.api.bund.dev/ not working (complete ABAP-Code is in post)

I had to import a certificate.
Also I made a typo when entering the URL in SM59

Thank you all for the support!

entry.title: Created SCORM content without Navigation but it is not working in iPhone
entry.content:
Dear All, 

I am facing an issue, I have created content in SAP SEN with the help of a Book Page. In that book page, I have maintained the control for no Navigation visualization to avoid backward and forward movement of the video, and the Video starts automatically, also maintained the required config for the Mobile screen as well as DEMO mode as well as book page. After exporting the content, scorm 1.2 Zip file got generated, that file needs to be uploaded to the LMS and assigned as a course. When that content is run on mobile, it is not run immediately need to tap on the screen, and after tapping, it works as expected. However, it does not work as expected on IPHONE; it shows the navigation, backward, and forward buttons and uses the player AIRPLAY.

Can anyone help with this? Why is this not working without TAP on screen in Android, and how to get rid of navigation in IPHONE

entry.title: DELTA-DTP error when loading data from 2LIS_11_VAITM
entry.content:
Hello,

we are using 2LIS_11_VAITM to extract sales document data to BW. Last night, when running our daily process chain, the DELTA DTP to store the data from PSA to DSO was running in an error. Sadly there is no error message or anything like this. What is wondering me: After filtering the datasets there are 0 datasets to be transferred to the DSO.. Is this the error? And how can i fix this?

Thanks for your help in advance.

Regards

Christian

entry.title: Re: Compensation - Promotion columns are not populate in worksheet after 1H 2025 Release
entry.content:
Hi @AndresCarter,

Thank you. I did open a ticket to SAP and they solved it by updating the relevant worksheet. They cannot find the route cause for this behavior. We don't understand why this happened because this worked all the time in the past as expected and we didn't change anything in the configuration. That is why we thought it's related to the 1H 2025 release and wanted to see if anyone else had this problem as well.

entry.title: Re: 8 Baby Steps to Learn Joule - (Step #3) Joule Architecture
entry.content:
@Raja_Gupta 
Could you show me which arrow in the diagram corresponds to which of the steps 1 to 9?

entry.title: Have you ever heard a child say: ‘I want to become an SAP Consultant?’
entry.content:
Let’s be honest: No one dreams of becoming an SAP Consultant when they’re a child.
There’s no LEGO set for it. No cartoon character. No poster on the classroom wall - no movie.

Five years ago, SAP was a complete mystery to me – or rather, a book with cryptic chapters: S/4HANA, ABAP, PP-DS, APO, IBP or BTP.

Back then, as a humanities graduate with art background, I would’ve loved to hear someone say:
“Go for it – your perspective belongs here, too.”

Today, I know: SAP is the perfect playground for those who want to shape, not just execute.
It’s full of complex systems – but also full of potential for human-centric change.
For people who don’t just think in transactions, but in transformation.

Working primarily in the German-speaking SAP ecosystem, I’ve seen both the opportunities and the blind spots. The strong structures – and the missing stories.

I’m currently writing my first book for SAP Press, planning a doctoral research project, and hosting two podcasts for the SAP community:
🎙 SAP Sommeliers – for everyone who enjoys SAP with depth and delight, and
🍵 Alice in Serverland – my teaparty for strong women in SAP.

Myself now in my office and when I was a child.

But most importantly: I’m here to help others not just get started – but to find and live their role in the SAP ecosystem in a self-determined and strength-oriented way.
Especially those who still think: “SAP? That’s not for me.”

Because here’s the truth:
In Germany, most young people – and especially women – don’t actively aim for a career in SAP. They stumble into it.
It’s rarely a conscious choice, even though the opportunities are enormous.

With a growing skills shortage and serious talent gaps, we can’t rely on coincidence anymore.
We need to turn accidental careers into intentional ones.
And that means: Every SAP consultant should speak about what they do – outside the SAP bubble.
To make this profession more tangible, more human, more alive.

Because maybe, just maybe, the next generation will say:
“I want to become an SAP Consultant – because I’ve seen what’s possible.”

💡 Let’s make SAP more approachable for young minds and career changers – and build the kind of ecosystem where dreams don’t have to start in tech to end in impact.

👉 What opened the door for you?
👉 Who helped you see your place in this ecosystem?
👉 Who should we make more visible in the SAP Community?

Let’s connect and co-create.

Warmly,
Sarah

P.S.: This was my Hello-World-Post for the SAP Community Blog - I hope you enjoyed it 🙂

entry.title: Re: Matrix Grid Reports: Performance-Potential (or How vs...
entry.content:
Hello Sofia,

I hope your issue might have been resolved.

Please check on the setting in the matrix grid : Performance-Potential (or How vs. What).

Check under the settings : make sure the setting is enabled Exclude deleted forms when retrieving ratings.

On the other hand :

Managers have updated people profile on employees with data in the live system. The issue is the employees not visible in the 9 Box rating of Hows VS what.

First check : Make sure the option for Matrix grid report is enabled in provisioning.

As per experience if this switch is disabled all the matrix grid settings will be disabled in the instance.

Second check:  Enable the setting on the Matrix grid Hows vs What - Use average of all found ratings, instead of latest - this will add all the ratings and average out for the employee.

 

Regards,

Aashi

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Analytic Model in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
We recently moved from SAP BW to SAP Datasphere, 

Basic capabilities like object-level access control are missing. If multiple models exist in a space, all are exposed to users there’s no option to restrict access to specific models with in a Space. This is a critical gap in governance and security. we will end up creating Hundreds of Space with the combination of models with Restriction.

The data loading monitor is another major shortcoming. It's cluttered and lacks visibility, making it nearly impossible to track DataLoads.

I must say—One Should Reconsider Replacing the BW with Datasphere without retaining BW as the backbone.

entry.title: Re: I it possible to export the SAP Enable Now extended s...
entry.content: @Anton_Mavrin Can I have the script that can change the pdf file name? We've been trying to find the solution for it, but so far .... nothing has found.

entry.title: Full Stack ABAP Application In Build
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to make a full stack ABAP Information using SAP Build For using AI Features like :

1) ABAP Business Object Generation.

2) ABAP Joule 

But Facing Some issues, Please Provide some assistance.

entry.title: Re: Can I extend a certain app? With Clean Core? How?
entry.content:
Hi @BartvdKamp 

What you are trying to do is unfortunately not possible. The errors you are getting are the obvious ones which you already identified yourself: the CDSs in question are not released for extension in ABAP Cloud, thus the clear "not released and thus not permitted" error.

But that isn't the final problem. Say all the CDS were released, what you are trying to do still won't work. You are trying to add a filter of another CDS entity into the root entity; this can never work as selections on the root entity happen on the root entity alone and if you wanted to filter on any association or child entity, that filter has to be passed correspondingly via that entity, not on the root. Here an example (I had no malfunction reports in my demo system, so I'm using Purchase Requisition to showcase my meaning):

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaserequisition_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaserequisition/0001/PurchaseReqn?$filter=_PurchaseRequisitionItem/any(a:a/MaterialGroup eq 'A001')

With this, the service returns all PR that have at least one item which has material group A001. The any operator only works on "to many" relationships, not 1:1. When you have a 1:1 you can access it directly via path expression, for example:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaserequisition_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaserequisition/0001/PurchaseReqnItem?$filter=_PurchaseReqnDelivAddress/Region eq 'CA'

All PR Items that have Region CA.

So you see, technically you could filter with _MaintenanceOrder/ScheduledBasicEndDate and _MaintenanceOrder/ScheduledBasicStartDate (as long as the OData service in question is built to support all regular OData protocol filtering - that could potentially be a hinderance as well, especially when we talk about SEGW services). 

So coming to, what you can do? A few ideas come to mind:

  1. First of, confirm how the OData query would look like, as in my examples, and test in a REST tool or directly on the GW client. Make sure that what you are trying to do is possible at all. If the OData service does not support the filtering you need, sadly you are out of luck. Then I can only refer to customer influence ticket to request the functionality you are missing.
  2. If 1. was successful, check with a developer if they can create an adaptation project for the SAP standard Fiori app and add custom filters that way, ensuring that either the "any" or path expression is done correctly, as you tested in 1. (refer to Filtering - Documentation - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK including how to do any). I'm not 100% sure this will work, but this is where I would try.
  3. If 1. and 2. didn't work, you could potentially looking at creating your own Fiori app, reusing the standard CDSs and adding again filtering via your Fiori app. I would only recommend this for pure read-access. If you need change/edit/delete etc. I do not recommend this as in a sense you are copying SAP standard functionality. If you need that, a potential workaround could be to navigate to the SAP standard app, once for example a malfunction report is clicked, and your app only takes care of the selection and list part. Not the nicest solution, but could be an option.

I hope this helps. This was a great question to showcase how every app and requirement can be a lot different 😀

entry.title: Can you pass sy-subrc as parameter in class constructor?
entry.content:
Is there a way to pass sy-subrc as parameter in class constructor?

Even when passing by value (both defualt and explicitly with subrc=sy-subrc)

constructor IMPORTING VALUE(subrc) TYPE syst_subrc DEFAULT sy-subrc

inside constructor both subrc and sy-subrc are 0. When calling opther methods it works as expected.

entry.title: Re: SAP Landscape Management Cloud discontinued
entry.content: Reply on behalf of Marco: I can only underline what Shaun has already said. From an SAP perspective, self-managed on a cloud provider is like on-premises. LaMa can continue to be used.

entry.title: Re: My blog does not get posted since 3 weeks
entry.content: ERP would be better

entry.title: Re: My blog does not get posted since 3 weeks
entry.content: As you are on it, I have another blog which is currently internally reviewed, so I did not start publishing yet. Could you please move this as well to ERP? It's https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogworkflowpage/blog-id/welcome-cornerblog-board/article-id/37036

entry.title: Re: EDIT HIRE RECORD BY IMPORT
entry.content:
Hi Hannah and everyone,
It is actually possible to edit a specific record via incremental import. In your import template csv file, you need to identify the record by providing the effective start date and sequence number, as well as the userId. This will result in an update of the record with this business key instead of creating a new record.
Best, Janina

entry.title: Voting needed for API Payment Advice (A2X) for including sub-items
entry.content:
Hello all,

I will appreciate your vote for the request raised :

API_PAYMENT_ADVICE_SRV - Payment Advice (A2X)

Request ID: 345910, Category: Finance
 

This API does not support Payment Advice Sub-Items in standard compared to the SOAP API Payment Advice - Create. 

Our requirement is to use the Odata API  for mass creation of Payment Advices. However our internal customers have business cases with sub-items payment advices is required.

By including Sub-items in Odata API, we can deliver mass creation solution for Payment Advices with Sub-Items.

Many thanks per advance !

entry.title: Re: Group Data Analysis (reporting logic) - ID Display Options
entry.content: did you see the options for compound or non compound key? This removes some of the path

entry.title: Create Maintenance Order App
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a requirement from my client. They want the plant operators to be able to create only one specific type of maintenance order — specifically, order type YA04 (Operational Maintenance).

I have configured restrictions for this order type only, but I’m still able to create other order types as well. While exploring options, I tried to adapt the UI of the "Create Maintenance Order" app to make the Order Type field read-only. However, the default values maintained in the Fiori settings are not being populated when I open the app.

In this context, I have a few questions:

  1. I have configured the restrictions generally in the roles BR_MAINTENANCE_PLANNER and BR_MAINTENANCE_TECHNICIAN, and I have also assigned the BR_EMPLOYEE_MAINTENANCE role. However, I haven’t been able to identify which catalog contains the Web Dynpro app "Create Maintenance Order". Because of this, I’m not sure whether I'm maintaining the restrictions correctly.

  2. Is it possible to create a variant from a Web Dynpro app?

  3. Is it possible for the fields in this app to be auto-filled using the default values?

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: SAC Story breaks when Datasphere model is updated
entry.content:
Hello,

This usually happens when a dimension/association/attribute has been unchecked and re-checked (added) at the analytic model level. After deployment, Datasphere will recreate a new internal ID for that dimension and it won't be recognized by the SAC story as the expected internal ID has changed for that object.

There might be other scenarios where that happens but that's the most common.

Regards,

Clément

entry.title: Through the Looking Glass: Manage Service Orders in Joule
entry.content:
Service Management and Joule have advanced their integration. After release of the first Joule case for service confirmations with OP2023 FPS3, the Joule release 04/25 now delivers first capabilities to manage service orders. We provide now a

  • Fast navigation to a service order
  • Search service orders
  • Release a single service order
  • Release a single service order item

Motivation and Scope

Joule is the central AI assistant provided by SAP. Joule enables users to execute tasks using fluent, easy-to-use language. The capabilities in Joule for managing service orders are a first basic step for a more simple, faster way to navigate, search and release single service orders and single service order items.

This is especially interesting for users like managers which are not very confident with the more detailed Manage Service Orders app in the Fiori Launchpad.

The capabilities for service orders are assigned to the role SAP_BR_SERVICE_MANAGER (Service Manager). The ODATA API for service orders which is called by Joule handles all authorizations as of now.

Starting Screen

The starting screen provides provides a button which opens up the Search Manage Order screen in the background.

 

Fast Navigation

Joule allows users to open service orders with simple prompts like ‘Display Service Order [Service Order ID]’. This feature displays essential details such as service order ID, description, and Sold-to-Party, with an ‘Open’ button for direct access.

If the button is chosen, the service order object page opens up directly.

Search Service Orders

Searching for service orders is now streamlined. Users can employ prompts like ‘Display service orders [search criterion] [value]’ to find orders based on criteria such as Posting Timeframe, Created By, and Changed At. 

The currently supported search criteria are:

  • Posting Timeframe
  • Created By (System User)
  • Created At
  • Changed By (System User)
  • Changed At

The search results are organized by the newest posting dates, enhancing efficiency. The button opens up the service order in the background as described in the navigational case.

The number of all displayed and existing service orders, e.g. 6/18 (6 out of 18 found) is displayed.

The five delivered search criteria—Posting Timeframe, Created by, Created at, Changed by, and Changed at—can be combined. But in the prompt currently a search criterion can be used only once.

A complex prompt example like ‘Display service orders created by VOLLMERMI and created at 11.05.2022 and changed by ZHANGBOWE1 and changed at 12.09.2022’.

The search with the criterion Posting Timeframe allows to search for a specific year, month or day.

Examples are posted last year, this year’, in 2022, current month, today, yesterday, etc.

 

Release of a Service Order

A prompt like ‘Release service order [service order ID]’ asks for confirmation to release this specific service order. If the user confirms, it checks first if the service order is already released.

If it is not released, Joule attempts to release this specific service order. After the execution, a feedback and the Open button for navigation is given. Below is an example of a successful release.

The example below shows an error case. The first error message of the transaction is displayed as feedback.

The example below shows a release of a service order which is already released. As can be seen, Joule checks this first and reacts accordingly.

Release of a Service Order Item

A prompt like ‘Release item [item number] of service order [service order ID].’ asks for confirmation to release this specific service order item. If the user confirms, it checks first if the service order item is already released.

If it is not released, Joule tries to release this specific service order item. After the execution, a feedback and the Open button for navigation is given. Below an example of a successful release is given.

The error case and an already released service order item are handled similar as with a service order release.

Capability Limitations

While Joule offers significant improvements, there are limitations, Currently there are the following limitations.

Direct Navigation:

Prompts like ‘Open up service order 800023831 directly’ are currently not supported. The provided open button must always be pressed. It is not possible to open up the service order object page via prompting only.

 

Search service orders:

Same criterion cannot be used multiple times. ‘Example: Search service orders created by VOLLMERMI and created by ZHANGBOWE1.’

 

Release of service orders and service order items:

Releasing multiple service orders in one prompt is currently not supported.

Releasing multiple service order items in one prompt is currently not supported

Release of service orders and service order items together in one prompt t is currently not supported.

Direct release without confirming the change. The current concept of Joule is that the user should get a summary of the change and has to confirm it before execution.

Joule is currently not able to differentiate between header and item errors. This means the release of a service order item is currently an error case as well if there is an error in the transaction on the header or another item.

 

Outlook

Future updates aim to expand search criteria. We also aim to add additional fields to the search result list. We are thinking as well about additional info sections to drill down for further information by another click. Last but not least additional status changes ands a create service order use case is looked upon.

Conclusion:

Joule is paving the way for a more intuitive and efficient service management experience. As SAP continues to refine and expand Joule's capabilities, users can look forward to even more streamlined processes. Your feedback is invaluable, so feel free to share your thoughts and suggestions.

Thank you for exploring the new features with us. We hope this makes your service management tasks a little easier and more enjoyable.

Last but not least if you liked this blog and found it helpful, I am happy to receive your kudos.

entry.title: Re: My blog does not get posted since 3 weeks
entry.content:
The first post has been published: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/through-the-looking-glass-manage-service-orders-in-joule/ba-p/14066461

And I have moved the second post to the ERP area.

entry.title: maintenance planner adds packages which not show dependant in SPAM
entry.content:
Dear Team, 

we are planning to update GRC system  to SP28 of grc components, we see sap note below requesting to update add-ons on dependent systems as well (S4hana, CAR, BW, Solman).

1352498 - Support Pack Numbering - GRC Access Control

when we see from spam we can update for dependent systems eg: S4hana only grc component update is possible (no update to other components like abap , basis gw, UI5). yet, when we generate stack from maintenance planner, we see many components getting update which will need more testing and remediation.

kindly suggest if we can update grc addon alone on remote systems and do SP update on GRC system.

entry.title: Re: How to know if the element is an OData entity
entry.content:
Hi,

This is a Gui screen and not a Fiori view. Data displayed on the screen might be via classical ABAP programming and not via OData.

Thanks

entry.title: how to create multiple GL accounts using template for Local Chart Of Account in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I want to create about 100 new GL Accounts in Local Chart of Account YBCZ.

We are using SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud 2502.

Could you please suggest how to create multiple GLs using template?

Thank you.

Regards,

Shrinivas

entry.title: Re: CPI to CPI Connection and both tenants will be on different subaccounts at different regions.
entry.content:
Hi @Yogita1 ,

There isn't any specific adapter to connect CPI systems, you can use standard adapters like HTTP/HTTPS. ProcessDirect adapter is only for iflows deployed in the same tenant.

Regards,

Roberto

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – What is Key User Extensibility and Who are your Key Users
entry.content:
Excellent blog Jocelyn! well done! 

We are currently in the explore phase of an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (2408) implementation for a public sector organization in the UK. During discussions around reporting in G/L, A/P, A/R, and procurement we’ve been informed that several existing reporting requirements are not supported by standard SAP Fiori apps and have therefore been flagged as custom requirements. Given the significant number of custom reports identified by the consultants, I wanted to clarify whether 'Key User Extensibility' is considered a configuration activity or classified as custom development. Our organization is committed to adhering to SAP’s 'Clean Core' strategy, so we would appreciate your guidance on this matter.

entry.title: Re: An error occurred while importing fixed asset posting...
entry.content:
Hello @yahaha 

To resolve this issue for ledger 0L, check and open the relevant periods in Asset Accounting for the specific depreciation area linked to ledger 0L:

Step-by-step solution (S/4HANA Public Cloud):

1.App: “Manage Fixed Asset Periods” (App ID: F1603)

◦ Use this app to open asset periods for the required company code and fiscal year.

2.Check the following:

◦ Ensure the company code is correct.

◦ Confirm that fiscal year 2025 is open for:

▪ Ledger 0L

The relevant depreciation area

◦ Make sure periods 01–12, or at least the current period, are marked as open.

3.App: “Define Depreciation Areas

◦ Verify which depreciation area is assigned to ledger 0L (e.g., area 01 or 10).

◦ Cross-check this with the information in the import error log.

4. Import again once all necessary periods are open in ledger 0L.

Note: Occasionally in S/4HANA Public Cloud, simulation runs may work in ledger 2L (e.g., for reporting), while 0L fails due to stricter local settings. Always use 0L as. your reference for period control.

Let me know if it works

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Save Time in SAP! Automate Repetitive Tasks with Script Recording - Test Data Creation
entry.content:
SAP consultants often face repetitive tasks, such as master data creation, test data creation, and transaction entries, which can be time-consuming and prone to errors when done manually.

Fortunately, SAP offers a powerful automation tool—SAP Script Recording—that allows users to record and replay repetitive processes, significantly reducing manual effort and improving accuracy. Whenever creating test data or updating material records is required, script recording can streamline these tasks with minimal coding knowledge.

In this blog post, we’ll explore how to leverage SAP Script Recording for automatic test data creation, including a step-by-step guide to recording, editing, and executing scripts in SAP.

Step 1: Enable Script Recording in SAP (Transaction: RZ11)

If the Current value is FALSE, mention the new value as "TRUE" and save.

Step 2: Let's create a Reservation Test Data (Transaction: MB21)

Click on Customise Local Layout and navigate to "Script Recording and Playback"

Complete the reservation creation process, and then click on SAP GUI Scripting and stop the process

You will find this option bottom right of the screen

Step 3: Adding a Condition to the Script

Open the Script file in Notepad, and add the condition as below

For I = 1 to 3

Means, if you run this script 3 times reservation creation process will be carried out.

Step 4: Running the Script in SAP

Navigate to transaction MB21 (reservation creation screen)

Select the Script file path and click on Resume Playback, and watch as the script executes automatically - like magic! 🎩✨

Step 5: Adding Iteration Text in Reservation

Now, in the Goods Recipient field text must be different in each reservation creation process. Update the condition as below.

Repeat Step 4 to run the script

SAP Script will create reservations with different texts for each reservation in the Goods Recipient field as below

 

Regards,

Leelamohan

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Daniel Mateo
entry.content:
Hello SAP family! This blog is a part of the "Blog It Forward" challenge. Thanks to my friend and almost neighbor @Lluis, and his recent blog, for tagging me to participate in this great initiative for the first time.

My name is Daniel Mateo, and I’m an experienced SAP S/4HANA Project Manager based in a coastal city near to Barcelona, with a passion for transforming business financial operations  leveraging SAP’s capabilities to create real business value and guide organizations through their digital transformation journeys.

I began my career around 20 years ago at a Spanish IT consulting firm as an ABAP developer, but I quickly transitioned into functional roles within Finance and Logistics. Over time, I specialized in international SAP rollouts, with a strong focus on Finance and Controlling. For the past 12 years, I have been working as an SAP Project Manager, leading global implementations and transformations across various sectors such as pharma, manufacturing, food and beverages, mass media, and casinos, among others. Throughout the years, I’ve had the privilege of working with diverse teams in multiple countries—combining my passion for travel with my career in SAP.

In addition to managing complex implementations, I thrive on continuous learning and adapting to the evolving landscape of SAP technologies. My journey has been shaped by both professional achievements and personal experiences, such as relocating to Bulgaria during the pandemic and staying for three years. This allowed me to embrace new challenges while contributing to SAP projects remotely.

Fun Facts About Me

  • 🌍SAP and Backpacking: A Perfect Match
    My career in SAP has taken me across the globe, and I have made the most of it by exploring over 60 countries. Whether it is backpacking through Southeast Asia, road-tripping across Europe, or discovering hidden gems in South America, I love immersing myself in new cultures. Traveling has also given me a unique perspective in my work—understanding different business environments, adapting to diverse teams, and experiencing firsthand how SAP solutions impact companies worldwide.
  • 🇧🇬Bulgaria: An Unexpected Home
    When the pandemic hit, I made the spontaneous decision to relocate to Bulgaria. What started as a temporary move turned into a three-year stay. I got to experience the country’s rich history, vibrant tech scene, and stunning nature, from the Black Sea coast to the Rila Mountains. It was an incredible time for personal and professional growth, and it reinforced how flexible and borderless the SAP world can be.
  • 🏖Yoga and Beach: Recharging My Energy
    No matter the season—winter or summer—I always find time to enjoy the beach. There's something about the sound of the waves and the fresh sea breeze that resets my mind after long hours on SAP projects. The perfect combination? Practicing yoga by the sea. It’s my go-to way to recharge and stay centered, especially when juggling tight go-lives, complex configurations, and international time zones. 

 What do you most enjoy about the SAP community?

The SAP community is an incredible ecosystem of professionals who are passionate about technology and innovation. What I enjoy the most is the collaborative spirit—whether it's through groups, events, or online discussions, there's always someone ready to share knowledge and best practices.

It is inspiring to be part of a global network that is constantly evolving and sharing the trending topics to enhance the business transformation.

I regularly participate in SAP Inside Track events (mainly in Fortaleza and Barcelona), both as a speaker and an attendee. In addition, I maintain an active presence on LinkedIn, where I share insights and experiences related to SAP. I am also the co-author of a book in Spanish that details the recommended Best Practices in SAP S/4HANA rollouts, further contributing to knowledge sharing within the ecosystem.

What source do you use the most for learning SAP technologies?

I rely on a combination of official SAP resources and community-driven content. SAP Learning Hub and SAP Help Portal are excellent for structured learning. Additionally, SAP blogs, SAP Community discussions, and LinkedIn groups provide real-world insights and practical applications from experienced professionals. Keeping up with industry leaders and engaging in discussions helps me stay ahead of emerging trends in the SAP ecosystem.

What do you think are the key elements for a successful project?

A successful SAP project is built on three fundamental pillars:

  1. Clear Business Objectives: Aligning technology with business goals ensures that the solution delivers real value.
  2. Strong Governance & Stakeholder Engagement: Continuous collaboration between IT and business teams minimizes risks and ensures smooth decision-making.
  3. Effective Change Management :The success of a project is not just about technical implementation; it’s about how well users adopt the system. Training, communication, and proactive issue resolution are critical for long-term success.

What do you enjoy most in your work and why?

What I enjoy the most is driving business transformation. Seeing how SAP solutions optimize financial processes, improve efficiency, and empower companies to make better decisions is incredibly rewarding. Every project is unique, with its own challenges and opportunities, and that variety keeps the work exciting. Also, working on international projects allows me to engage with diverse teams and cultures, which is a great learning experience.

How do you survive when SAP keeps shifting our journey to new products?

SAP’s ecosystem is constantly evolving, and while it can feel overwhelming (especially with all the new acronyms, product renamings, and shifting roadmaps), the key to surviving is to position yourself as someone who doesn’t just follow the change, but helps lead it.

Here are a few survival strategies:

  1. Stay Curious and Keep Learning: New product? New cloud solution? New “RISE with” slogan? Dive into it. Take SAP Learning Hub courses, read blog posts, join SAP Community discussions. Even if you don’t implement it tomorrow, understanding what’s coming is power.

  2. Stick to Core Principles: While tools change, the fundamentals of business processes, integration, data governance, and project management stay solid. Focus on what delivers value to the business—technology is just the enabler.

  3. Build a Solid Network: Talk to other professionals, share experiences, learn from what’s worked (or not worked) in other projects. LinkedIn, SAP Community, and internal project retrospectives can be goldmines.

  4. Be Honest with Clients and Stakeholders: Don’t pretend to have all the answers. SAP sometimes doesn’t either! But if you can bring clarity to the ambiguity, people will trust you even more.

  5. Have a Sense of Humor: When a product name changes for the third time in a year, laugh. Then update your slide deck.

How do you see SAP technology in the next 5 years?

SAP technology is evolving rapidly, and I see three key trends shaping its future:

  1. Cloud-First & AI-Driven Solutions: SAP will continue pushing towards cloud-based ERP with AI-driven automation to enhance decision-making.

  2. Increased Integration & Interoperability: With the rise of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), we will see more seamless integration between SAP and non-SAP applications.

  3. Sustainability & Compliance: SAP is heavily investing in sustainability solutions, and I expect more tools helping companies track and manage their Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.

I hope you enjoy reading this blog as much as I enjoyed sharing my journey. Thanks for being part of the SAP community!

I would like to Blog it forward to @Marssel700 ,  a well-established SAP Champion with a strong finance background and creator of amazing courses, and to @AAncos , one of the top voices in the SAP Spanish-speaking community who truly deserves to be part of the SAP champions. I would suggest answering the question: With SAP's continuous push toward cloud-based solutions, how do you see the role of traditional SAP consultants evolving over the next 3–5 years? I'm sure they both have plenty of surprising insights and stories to share with us! 😉

entry.title: Re: ABAP for Cloud Development & Package interfaces
entry.content:
Hello Thomas / @ThFiedler,

is there any news regarding this topic? Do i understand correctly that "making objects available" is only possible for all or nothing outside current package, as C1 is "system internally" and there is nothing more fine granular?

Best regards

Robert
